Title: Major Facilitator Superfamily transporters balance sugar metabolism in peach

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: BG5; KX_1729_93; wen_07-2-30 × wen_07-2-47 hybrid population; 23 natural population lines
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Major Facilitator Superfamily; tonoplastic sugar transporter (TST/TMT subfamily)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Sucrose; fructose; glucose; sorbitol; soluble solids content (SSC)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit flesh, especially outer flesh during ripening; vacuole/tonoplast-associated sugar storage
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines fruit sweetness, consumer preference, and market value
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes sugar accumulation/tonoplast import but higher preripening expression and Hap2 are associated with lower SSC; balances sugar contents and SSC in a stage-dependent manner
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression increases during fruit development and ripening; significantly upregulated from premature to mature stages; enriched in outer flesh during ripening
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed during peach fruit maturation in DG, ZB, and BG5; upregulated in KX_1729_93 mature fruit tissues; expression differs by upstream haplotype in natural populations
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; spatial metabolomics (MALDI-MSI); HPLC metabolite profiling; QTL mapping; resequencing/SNP haplotyping; SRA PRJNA1054471; GSA BioProject 2537–2542
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Zheng Bai 5-2; Tianjin Shuimi; Lovell 2.0 reference
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage (premature vs mature/ripening); tissue position (inner, middle, outer flesh)
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: NA; paper hypothesizes possible interaction of upstream variants with transcription factors or epigenetic modifiers but provides no direct evidence
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in peach fruit; RT-qPCR; HPLC/SSC phenotyping; QTL mapping; haplotype association; expression-phenotype correlation; prior functional characterization cited
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpTST1 is a major MFS transporter associated with sugar accumulation during peach fruit ripening. 2. Its expression increases during development and is positively associated with sucrose accumulation in transcriptomic/spatial analyses. 3. QTL mapping identified a major upstream locus on chromosome 5 linked to PpTST1. 4. Upstream haplotypes fine-tune PpTST1 expression and are associated with fructose, glucose, and SSC variation. 5. PpTST1 balances sugar content and SSC in a context- and stage-dependent manner.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for marker-assisted selection of favorable upstream haplotypes and for tuning expression to optimize sugar accumulation without undesirable SSC imbalance; timing of expression should be considered in engineering
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increased sugar content in prior work but did not improve SSC in this study; possible compensatory changes in organic acid metabolism or water content
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats PpTST1 as the key QTL gene. Hap1 is associated with lower PpTST1 expression and higher sugar/SSC, whereas Hap2 associates with higher expression and lower sugar/SSC, indicating developmental timing/context dependence.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Major Facilitator Superfamily; ERD6-like sugar transporter
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit flesh, especially ripening/outer flesh-associated sucrose accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes sucrose accumulation and transport into the injected region during ripening
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during fruit maturation; significantly upregulated from premature to mature stages
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Positively correlated with sucrose content in BG5_3 and BG5_4; upregulated in ripening fruit
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; spatial metabolomics; HPLC metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; tissue spatial position
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression and VIGS in peach fruit; RT-qPCR; HPLC sugar quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpERDL16-1 expression is positively correlated with sucrose accumulation during peach fruit ripening. 2. It is significantly upregulated from premature to mature stages. 3. Overexpression promotes movement of sugars from uninjected to injected tissue and favors sucrose accumulation locally.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to enhance peach fruit sweetness through promoting sucrose accumulation during ripening
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of the main ripening-associated MFS genes with positive effect on sucrose accumulation.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Major Facilitator Superfamily; ERD6-like sugar transporter
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to sugar partitioning during fruit development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generally reduces local sucrose accumulation in OE assays; implicated in sugar partitioning/compartmentalization
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Positively correlated with sucrose content; upregulated in premature-stage tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Commonly upregulated across inner, middle, and outer regions at premature stage in KX_1729_93
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage and tissue region
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression and VIGS in peach fruit; RT-qPCR; HPLC sugar quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpERDL6 shows positive transcript correlation with sucrose content but functional assays indicate complex transport effects. 2. OE moved sugars from injected to uninjected tissue. 3. VIGS reversed sucrose transport direction relative to other sugars, supporting a role in sugar partitioning.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modulating sugar partitioning, but complex effects require caution
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduced sugar levels according to discussion; transport effects are substrate-dependent
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role appears more complex than simple positive/negative regulation of sweetness.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Major Facilitator Superfamily; polyol/monosaccharide transporter (PMT/PLT/SFP subfamily)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects fruit sweetness and soluble solids
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts mainly as a negative regulator of sucrose accumulation during ripening, though OE locally promoted sucrose accumulation in injected tissue and significantly increased SSC
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed and significantly downregulated from premature to mature stages
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly significant negative correlation with sucrose content during BG5_3–BG5_4; downregulated during ripening in KX_1729_93
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage and tissue region
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression and VIGS in peach fruit; RT-qPCR; HPLC sugar quantification; SSC measurement
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpPMT5-1 is negatively correlated with sucrose during ripening. 2. It is downregulated from premature to mature stages. 3. OE caused transport of all measured sugars from uninjected to injected tissue and promoted local sucrose accumulation. 4. PpPMT5-1 OE was the only tested gene to significantly increase SSC.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for increasing SSC, but sugar-component and developmental effects are complex and should be optimized carefully
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contrasting effects on sucrose correlation versus transient OE response suggest context dependence
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Adjacent to PpPMT5-2 on chromosome 8 and belongs to the same family.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Major Facilitator Superfamily; polyol/monosaccharide transporter (PMT/PLT/SFP subfamily)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to sugar partitioning relevant to fruit sweetness
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces local sucrose accumulation; OE/VIGS both associated with net sugar transport from injected to uninjected tissue
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed; significantly negatively correlated with sucrose content
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Negative correlation with sucrose content across BG5 developmental stages
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression and VIGS in peach fruit; RT-qPCR; HPLC sugar quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpPMT5-2 is negatively associated with sucrose content during peach fruit development. 2. Functional assays indicate it drives export-like movement of sugars from injected to uninjected tissue. 3. It likely contributes to reduced sucrose retention in flesh.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for suppressing to retain more sugar in fruit tissues
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Analyzed because of strong expression and correlation, but less emphasized than PpTST1, PpERDL16-1, PpPMT5-1, and PpSUT4.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sucrose transporter family (SUT/SUC); Major Facilitator Superfamily
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively affects sucrose accumulation during ripening; OE reduced sugars in injected tissue while silencing reversed sucrose/sorbitol transport direction
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed; significantly downregulated during ripening
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly significant negative correlation with sucrose content during BG5_3–BG5_4; downregulated in mature-stage tissues
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage and tissue region
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression and VIGS in peach fruit; RT-qPCR; HPLC sugar quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpSUT4 is negatively correlated with sucrose accumulation during ripening. 2. It is downregulated from premature to mature stages. 3. OE transported sugars from injected to uninjected tissue, reducing local sugar retention, while VIGS reversed transport direction for sucrose and sorbitol.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Silencing or downregulation may help improve sugar retention in fruit flesh
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the main negatively associated transporters highlighted by both transcriptomics and functional assays.
